name: B-Mesogenesis
summary: Couples baryogenesis and dark-matter production to the standard B-meson system. A long-lived heavy particle decays out of thermal equilibrium into B0 and anti-B0 mesons; the mesons oscillate (a CP-violating process measured at LHCb and Belle II) and then decay to a visible baryon plus a dark-sector anti-baryon. Total baryon number is conserved across visible + dark sectors. Directly testable through specific B-decay channels at hadron colliders and B-factories.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces eta ~ 6e-10 for heavy-particle masses near the GeV-TeV scale and B-meson CP asymmetries within current LHCb/Belle bounds. Falsifiable: predicts BR(B0 -> baryon + dark) at the 10^-7 - 10^-4 level testable at LHCb Run 3 and Belle II.

Dark sector anti-baryon (a stable baryon-charged dark particle) constitutes the DM; abundance is linked to the BAU through the same B-decay branching ratios. Compelling for testability: the same parameters fix BAU and Omega_DM.

touches_benchmarks.note: B-mesogenesis exploits SM B-mixing CP violation; consistent with current measurements but provides additional CP-violating phases to be searched for.
Dark sector anti-baryon is feebly coupled to the visible SM through B-meson decays only; direct-detection cross-section far below LZ sensitivity.

new_fields: scalar (or fermion) Phi mediator that couples to b-quark and dark baryon
introduces.note: Mediator mass, dark-baryon mass, CP-violating phases, mediator-SM coupling.

history: Elor, Escudero, Nelson (2018), 'Baryogenesis and dark matter from B mesons', Phys. Rev. D 99, 035031 (2019), arXiv:1810.00880. Phenomenology refined by Alonso-Alvarez-Elor-Escudero (2021, arXiv:2101.02706); LHCb and Belle II are actively pursuing the predicted B-decay channels.

references.citation: Elor, Escudero, Nelson, 'Baryogenesis and dark matter from B mesons', Phys. Rev. D 99, 035031 (2019)
Alonso-Alvarez, Elor, Escudero, 'Collider signals of baryogenesis and dark matter from B mesons', Phys. Rev. D 104, 035028 (2021)
